<!-- AUTOGENERATED from spec/src/tensio + spec/content — do not edit by hand. Edits: docstrings/content -> uv run python tools/gen_spec.py -->

# Substrate atoms

The atomic requirements that govern how the substrate (graph + generated docs) behaves.

---

## `R-claude-md-live-state-generated` (ENFORCED)

**Claim.** The live numeric state in CLAUDE.md (top action, debt counts, graph size, crystal headroom, context) shall be generated by gen_spec into a sentinel-delimited block, never hand-written.

**Why.** Commit 36ceabd hand-wrote 'Today: 15 unenforced' into CLAUDE.md — the auto-loaded file — and it drifted to 16 within one phase. The U5 lesson (single source + generated mirror) applied to the operator's own crystal. gen_spec is the 'hook that updates it with the logic run'.

**Enforced by:** `test_docs_gen.py::test_claude_md_live_state_up_to_date`, `tools/gen_spec.py`

## `R-content-free-framework` (ENFORCED)

**Claim.** spec/src/tensio/ shall contain ZERO business content — no example requirements, no example axes, no seed graph.

**Why.** Tensio is a blank kit. Business content lives under spec/content/; the worked example is a test fixture. REPLACES the earlier design where seed data lived in src/tensio/graph.py.

**Enforced by:** `test_content_free.py`

## `R-deterministic-generation` (ENFORCED)

**Claim.** tools/gen_spec.py shall produce byte-stable LF utf-8 output with no timestamps or randomness — two runs over an unchanged graph yield identical bytes.

**Why.** Determinism is what makes the anti-drift meta-test possible. Without it, regeneration would never equal the committed file.

**Enforced by:** `test_docs_gen.py::test_generator_is_deterministic`

## `R-drift-structurally-impossible` (ENFORCED)

**Claim.** The generated docs/gen/*.md shall equal the regeneration of the current spec/content + framework docstrings, byte-for-byte.

**Why.** Anti-drift meta-test (tests/test_docs_gen.py) — direct lift of dev-coin's pattern. The human layer cannot be hand-edited.

**Enforced by:** `test_docs_gen.py::test_requirements_md_up_to_date`, `test_docs_gen.py::test_tensions_md_up_to_date`, `test_docs_gen.py::test_open_md_up_to_date`, `test_docs_gen.py::test_unenforced_md_up_to_date`

## `R-rejected-preserved-not-deleted` (PROSE)

**Claim.** Requirements that are rejected shall be marked REJECTED and kept in the graph for history, never deleted.

**Why.** Anti-relitigation. Without preserved REJECTED, the same dead ideas re-surface every quarter. The historian role depends on this preservation.
